title: Poor Separation of Concerns
summary: Business logic, infrastructure code, UI behavior, and data access are mixed together.
inspection_goal: Detect architectural areas where concerns that should remain distinct are structurally entangled.
why_it_matters: Raises change risk and side effects
primary_signals: UI-facing modules depend directly on persistence layers
Domain modules depend directly on framework or transport code
Infrastructure modules appear on critical business paths
Many layers collapse into direct edges without mediation
inspection_questions: Are domain concerns isolated from infrastructure details?
Do modules cross several architectural layers directly?
Where are boundaries between application, domain, and data access blurred?
evidence_to_collect: Dependency edges between mismatched concern types
Hotspots where multiple concern categories intersect
recommendation_patterns: Re-layer components to reduce cross-layer leakage

severity_guidance.high: Layering is broadly collapsed and business logic depends directly on low-level details.
severity_guidance.medium: Concern mixing is localized but structurally visible.
severity_guidance.low: Most layers are separated, with only isolated leaks.
